prompt: Show me my recent activity
expected_output: Agent runs kpass activity --output json and renders each event as a display card with kind, status, timestamp, and relevant details

prompt: Show me only my wallet transfers
expected_output: Agent runs kpass activity --kind wallet_transfer --output json and renders wallet transfer events as display cards with amount, asset, and tx hash

prompt: I can see 10 events on the first page of my activity — show me the next page of older activity
expected_output: Agent runs kpass activity --limit N --offset N --output json to paginate results beyond the first page, using an offset equal to the number of events already shown

prompt: Log me in to Kite Passport
expected_output: Agent runs setup.sh, then kpass login init --email <EMAIL> --output json --no-interactive, asks the user for the OTP code, then runs kpass login verify

prompt: I need to create a Kite Passport account
expected_output: Agent runs setup.sh, then kpass signup init --email <EMAIL> --output json --no-interactive, waits for email verification, then runs signup poll and signup exchange

prompt: Am I logged in?
expected_output: Agent runs kpass me --output json and reports the current user email and user_id if authenticated, or tells the user they are not logged in

prompt: Create an agent session to access api.example.com
expected_output: Agent constructs a delegation JSON with task description, paymentPolicy with max_amount_per_tx and max_total_amount, and executionPolicy, then passes it to kpass agent:session create

prompt: Set up a session with a $5 budget to call the Exa search API at exa.mpp.tempo.xyz
expected_output: Agent preflights exa.mpp.tempo.xyz to discover payment requirements, then constructs a delegation with max_total_amount reflecting the $5 total budget and max_amount_per_tx set to the per-request price, then calls kpass agent:session create

prompt: Find paid APIs for weather data
expected_output: Agent runs setup-ksearch.sh, then ksearch services list --query weather --output json, and renders each service as a display card with name, summary, pricing, and base URL

prompt: Show me details about the Exa service
expected_output: Agent runs ksearch services get --service-id <exa-service-id> --output json and renders the service detail card with all endpoints, pricing, and payment approach

prompt: What paid services are available on Kite?
expected_output: Agent runs ksearch services list --output json and displays all available services with name, summary, starting price, and tags

prompt: What agents are registered on my account?
expected_output: Agent runs kpass user agents --output json and displays a list of registered agents with their IDs, types, and registration dates

prompt: Show me my active spending sessions
expected_output: Agent runs kpass user sessions --output json and displays each session with its status, delegation details, and spending limits

prompt: Set up a spending session so I can make payments
expected_output: Agent runs kpass agent:register --type claude --output json to register the agent, then constructs a delegation and runs kpass agent:session create, then opens the approval URL for the user

prompt: Use my existing session to make a payment
expected_output: Agent runs kpass agent:session list --output json first to find an active session, then proceeds with the payment execution using the existing session_id

prompt: Check the status of my pending session
expected_output: Agent runs kpass agent:session list --output json and shows session status, delegation details, and whether the session is pending, active, or expired

prompt: Find me a phone charger under $10
expected_output: Agent runs kpass shop:search "phone charger" --output json with max-results, displays product cards with title, price, and provider, then asks which to add to cart

prompt: What's in my shopping cart?
expected_output: Agent runs kpass shop:cart --output json and renders each cart item as a display card with title, quantity, price, and provider, then shows the cart total

prompt: I want to checkout and buy the items in my cart
expected_output: Agent verifies shipping info with kpass shop:shipping --output json, confirms the cart total with the user, creates a spending session with a budget matching the total, then runs kpass shop:checkout --output json only after explicit user confirmation

prompt: What's my wallet balance?
expected_output: Agent runs kpass wallet balance --output json and displays a card showing wallet address, chain, and each asset symbol with its balance

prompt: Send 5 USDC to 0xabc123def456
expected_output: Agent runs kpass wallet send --to 0xabc123def456 --asset USDC --amount 5 --output json and displays a confirmation card with the tx hash

prompt: Make a paid API call to api.example.com/data
expected_output: Agent runs kpass agent:session execute --url api.example.com/data --output json and displays the response content from the paid API endpoint

prompt: POST to api.example.com/submit with body {"key": "value"}
expected_output: Agent runs kpass agent:session execute --url api.example.com/submit --method POST --body '{"key": "value"}' --output json

prompt: Log me out of Kite Passport
expected_output: Agent runs kpass logout --output json and confirms the user has been signed out

prompt: I tried to log in but got an error saying my email isn't registered — what should I do?
expected_output: Agent recognises exit code 4 / 'email not registered' and falls back to kpass signup init --email <EMAIL> --no-interactive --output json, then continues the signup flow with signup poll and signup exchange

prompt: The OTP code I entered didn't work — it says invalid code
expected_output: Agent recognises the invalid OTP error (exit code 3 from login verify), asks the user to re-check their email for the correct 8-character code, and retries kpass login verify with the same login_id

prompt: Drop some testnet USDC into my wallet
expected_output: Agent runs kpass faucet drop --recipient <ADDRESS> --token USDC --output json and displays a Tokens Received card with the drop details

prompt: Send 0.5 ETH to 0xdeadbeef1234567890abcdef
expected_output: Agent runs kpass wallet send --to 0xdeadbeef1234567890abcdef --asset ETH --amount 0.5 --output json and shows the Transfer Complete card

prompt: Show me only my Claude agents
expected_output: Agent runs kpass user agents --agent-type claude --output json and lists only agents of type claude

prompt: List only my active sessions
expected_output: Agent runs kpass user sessions --status active --output json and shows only sessions with active status, including remaining budget from usage fields

prompt: I approved the session in my Passport app — is it active yet?
expected_output: Agent runs kpass agent:session status --request-id <REQUEST_ID> --wait --output json to poll for approval and reports the result once the session becomes active

prompt: Register my agent before setting up a session
expected_output: Agent runs kpass agent:register --type claude --output json and displays the registration confirmation with the new agent_id

prompt: Add a wireless mouse to my cart
expected_output: Agent runs kpass shop:search "wireless mouse" to find a product, then runs kpass shop:cart add --provider <PROVIDER> --external-id <ID> --quantity 1 --output json and shows the Added to Cart confirmation card

prompt: Remove the keyboard from my cart
expected_output: Agent runs kpass shop:cart view to identify the keyboard item, then runs kpass shop:cart remove --provider <PROVIDER> --external-id <ID> --output json

prompt: Empty my shopping cart
expected_output: Agent runs kpass shop:cart clear --output json and confirms the cart has been cleared

prompt: Update my shipping address to 123 Main St, Springfield, IL 62701
expected_output: Agent runs kpass shop:shipping update with --name, --email, --line1, --city, --state, --postal flags populated and --output json

prompt: What's the status of my order ORD-abc123?
expected_output: Agent runs kpass shop:order status --order-id ORD-abc123 --output json and reports the current order status and tracking info

prompt: Find AI APIs on Kite — I only want ones that accept USDC
expected_output: Agent runs ksearch services list --query AI --asset USDC --output json (or equivalent filter combination) to surface services accepting USDC payment

prompt: Show me more Kite services — there must be more than the first page
expected_output: Agent runs ksearch services list --limit N --cursor <CURSOR> --output json using the cursor from the previous response to fetch the next page

prompt: Is the Kite service catalog working right now?
expected_output: Agent runs ksearch health --output json and reports whether the catalog backend is reachable and healthy

prompt: PUT to api.example.com/resource/42 with body {"status": "done"}
expected_output: Agent runs kpass agent:session execute --url api.example.com/resource/42 --method PUT --body '{"status": "done"}' --output json

prompt: Call api.example.com/ingest with a custom header X-Api-Version: 2
expected_output: Agent runs kpass agent:session execute --url api.example.com/ingest --headers '{"X-Api-Version": "2"}' --output json with the header passed as a JSON object

prompt: The paid API returned a 404 — does that mean my payment failed?
expected_output: Agent explains that a non-2xx status in x402.status_code is a response from the target service, not a payment failure — the CLI exits 0 and payment was processed. It shows the x402.status_code field and advises checking the target API's documentation.

prompt: How much of my session budget have I used so far?
expected_output: Agent runs kpass agent:session list --status active --output json and reads usage.spent_total vs payment_policy.max_total_amount to show remaining budget

prompt: Set up a session for api.payments.xyz — I don't know what it costs yet
expected_output: Agent performs a preflight curl to api.payments.xyz to discover 402 payment requirements, displays the Payment Requirements Discovered card, then constructs a delegation and shows the Proposed Session Parameters card before asking for confirmation

prompt: The session approval request I sent expired before I approved it
expected_output: Agent recognises the expired session request error (exit code 3, session request expired) and creates a new session with kpass agent:session create, presenting a new approval URL

prompt: Show me my x402 API payment history
expected_output: Agent runs kpass activity --kind x402_payment --output json and renders each API payment event as a display card showing the endpoint, amount, and timestamp

prompt: Show me my shopping checkouts from last month
expected_output: Agent runs kpass activity --kind shopping_checkout --output json with offset pagination to reach older events, then filters by occurred_at to surface events within the requested time window

prompt: Search for AI services in the Kite catalog and show me the details for the first result
expected_output: Agent runs ksearch services list --query AI --output json to get results, extracts the first service_id, then runs ksearch services get --service-id <ID> --output json to show full details

prompt: I want to buy a USB hub and a webcam in one order
expected_output: Agent searches for each product, runs kpass shop:cart add for each item, shows the full cart, verifies shipping, creates a single spending session sized to the combined total, and only runs kpass shop:checkout --confirmed after explicit user approval

prompt: Set up a session for fal.ai with $0.03 per-tx and $3 total budget
expected_output: Agent preflights fal.ai, derives the payment approach from the 402 response (likely tempo), and calls kpass agent:session create --delegation '<INNER-JSON>' --output json. The inner JSON must contain payment_policy.max_amount_per_tx, payment_policy.max_total_amount, and payment_policy.allowed_payment_approaches with the derived approach. Amount strings must be CLEAN decimals (e.g. 0.03, not 0.029999999999999998) — no more than 6 decimal digits and no trailing-9 float artifacts — so the USDC 6-decimal check does not reject the value at execute time.

prompt: Create a session for a service that uses tempo payment protocol
expected_output: Agent runs kpass agent:session create --delegation '<INNER-JSON>' --output json where payment_policy.allowed_payment_approaches is ["tempo"] (the base name — never tempo_http). Must not pass an _http-suffixed value in the allowed_payment_approaches array.

prompt: I created a session in the Passport dashboard, here's the ID: session_dash7788. Use it so you can make payments.
expected_output: Agent registers itself if needed, then runs kpass agent:session attach --session-id session_dash7788 --output json directly — no merchant preflight, no delegation JSON construction, since the owner already defined the session's policy. Displays the Attach Approval Required card with the approval_url, opens it, and polls kpass agent:session status --request-id <id> --wait --output json until the owner approves, then shows the Session Attached card with the task summary and budget read from the approved session's delegation.

prompt: Attach session_dedicated4421 to this agent — I already have it.
expected_output: Agent runs kpass agent:session attach --session-id session_dedicated4421 --output json, which returns exit code 2 with error_code: session_not_attachable because the session is a dedicated (create-time-bound) session, not an attachable one. Agent explains the distinction and points to request-session for creating a new session, rather than retrying attach on the same ID.

prompt: Attach session_foreignowner902 to this agent.
expected_output: Agent runs kpass agent:session attach --session-id session_foreignowner902 --output json, which returns exit code 6 with error_code: agent_not_same_owner because the session belongs to a different account's agent. Agent explains the ownership boundary and does not retry with a different fabricated --agent-id or suggest re-registering as a workaround.
